creator: matthieu.volat@ujf-grenoble.fr
text:
Le 14 févr. 2014 à 22:18, Tijl Coosemans <tijl@FreeBSD.org> a écrit :

> Hi,
> 
> I only discovered this PR in my email trash folder after having committed
> my own net/mpich port.
> 
> Can you check out the port and tell me if anything is missing?
> 
> I committed 3.1rc3 which compiles with clang so your patches for weak
> symbols aren't needed.
> Looks like I'm missing CONFLICTS=mpich2-[0-9]*.
> Do you use gforker?


Hi, and thanks for your work and notification.

Yes, I use gforker for development and quick tests at the scale of one machine. I suppose the ideal would be a full choice of process managers, complete with the default one, but I didn't have enough time to tackle that.

However, I see great interest in not forcing fortran, a lot of people don't use it for MPI (thus not needing gcc), and I did not see any bash/perl specifics in the sources tools (I built my version in a clean VM).

--
Matthieu Volat <matthieu.volat@ujf-grenoble.fr>
Ingénieur de Recherche CNRS
ISTerre - Institut des Sciences de la Terre
Université Joseph Fourier, Grenoble
